meta.setting: Snow-dusted Klamath mountain ridge above stock country
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in rare predator sign and rumor pressure

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Gray Wolf
research_grounding.use: Gray wolves as native but rare/disappearing by the early 20th century, suitable for uncertain 1899 sign.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Deer sign as prey and track comparison.

image_prompt.subject: a snow-dusted ridge trail with possible wolf tracks crossing deer sign while ranch riders argue in the distance under a hard gray sky
image_prompt.scene: winter edge of the Klamath mountains; thin snow, pine shadows, nervous cattle far below, a ranch dog sniffing the tracks, and no clear pack attack
image_prompt.composition: square quiet landscape with tracks in sharp foreground and human dispute small in background; evidence first, people second
image_prompt.camera: low track-level angle, early-HD ground texture, with riders framed far enough away to feel uncertain
material_cues: deer tracks crossing at an angle
gray pine bark and cold sky
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the uncertainty the center: nobody in the image should fully agree what they are seeing.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine tracking and rumor scene
render_traits: slightly simplified rider, dog, cattle, and track silhouettes
modest texture resolution on snow, dirt, wool, wood, and pine
hard ambient occlusion in track depressions and under horse legs
cold gray winter light with low contrast
quiet evidence-forward composition rather than attack spectacle
style_modifiers.palette: snow gray, frozen dirt umber, pine black-green, wool brown, rifle wood, dog tan, cold sky blue, and distant cattle dark.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, tracking overlays, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The possible wolf tracks, deer sign, riders, dog, and winter ridge are fully readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 ranch and trail life.
The image centers uncertainty rather than a clear predator attack.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
